evals.name: add-logging-to-service-create
evals.prompt: Add appropriate logging to ClientService.create — we're losing track of who created what and when in the logs. Don't go overboard.
evals.expected_output: Adds @Slf4j to the service. One INFO log after successful save with structured fields (clientId, maybe firstName as a non-PII identifier — debatable, mentioned as trade-off). Does NOT log email, phoneNumber, or the full request body. Does NOT add DEBUG noise to every method. Does NOT log-and-rethrow on exceptions (GlobalExceptionHandler already does that).

evals.name: audit-global-exception-handler
evals.prompt: Review GlobalExceptionHandler's logging. Is anything missing for ops? What should we add or change?
evals.expected_output: Accurately describes current behavior: 5xx logged at ERROR with stack trace, 4xx (handled exceptions) logged at INFO with message only, bean-validation 400s not logged at all, correlationId generated per error but returned only in ApiError (never pushed to MDC). Flags gaps like: correlationId not in MDC (so upstream request logs can't be grouped — recommend a filter), handleMethodArgumentNotValid silently drops validation failures (hard to diagnose 400 spikes), INFO for 4xx may under-warn on genuinely unexpected client errors, no standardized structured field names. Ranks by severity.

evals.name: pii-leak-audit
evals.prompt: Security flagged that we might be logging email addresses or phone numbers somewhere. Audit our logging statements across the codebase and flag any PII leaks. Recommend fixes.
evals.expected_output: Greps for log statements referencing email, phoneNumber, lastName across the codebase. For each finding, classifies severity (definite PII, possibly scrub-safe, unclear). Fixes either replace the PII field with clientId or remove it entirely from the log line. Does NOT blanket-disable the logs.
